NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. To You, O LORD, I lift up my soul.
NKJV.2: O my God, I trust in You; Let me not be ashamed;  Let not my enemies triumph over me.
NKJV.3: Indeed, let no one who waits on You be ashamed; Let those be ashamed who deal treacherously without cause.
NKJV.4: Show me Your ways, O LORD; Teach me Your paths.
NKJV.5: Lead me in Your truth and teach me, For You are the God of my salvation; On You I wait all the day.
NKJV.6: Remember, O LORD, Your tender mercies and Your lovingkindnesses, For they are from of old.
NKJV.7: Do not remember the sins of my youth, nor my transgressions;  According to Your mercy remember me, For Your goodness’ sake, O LORD.
NKJV.8: Good and upright is the LORD; Therefore He teaches sinners in the way.
NKJV.9: The humble He guides in justice, And the humble He teaches His way.
NKJV.10: All the paths of the LORD are mercy and truth, To such as keep His covenant and His testimonies.
NKJV.11: For Your name’s sake, O LORD, Pardon my iniquity, for it is great.
NKJV.12: Who is the man that fears the LORD? Him shall He teach in the way He chooses.
NKJV.13: He himself shall dwell in prosperity, And his descendants shall inherit the earth.
NKJV.14: The secret of the LORD is with those who fear Him, And He will show them His covenant.
NKJV.15: My eyes are ever toward the LORD, For He shall pluck my feet out of the net.
NKJV.16: Turn Yourself to me, and have mercy on me, For I am desolate and afflicted.
NKJV.17: The troubles of my heart have enlarged; Bring me out of my distresses!
NKJV.18: Look on my affliction and my pain, And forgive all my sins.
NKJV.19: Consider my enemies, for they are many; And they hate me with cruel hatred.
NKJV.20: Keep my soul, and deliver me; Let me not be ashamed, for I put my trust in You.
NKJV.21: Let integrity and uprightness preserve me, For I wait for You.
NKJV.22: Redeem Israel, O God, Out of all their troubles!

KJV.1: A Psalm of David<S>1732</S>. Unto thee, O LORD<S>3068</S>, do I lift up<S>5375</S> my soul<S>5315</S>.
KJV.2: O my God<S>430</S>, I trust<S>982</S> in thee: let me not be ashamed<S>954</S>, let not mine enemies<S>341</S> triumph<S>5970</S> over me.

KJV.5: Lead<S>1869</S> me in thy truth<S>571</S>, and teach<S>3925</S> me: for thou art the God<S>430</S> of my salvation<S>3468</S>; on thee do I wait<S>6960</S> all the day<S>3117</S>.

KJV.7: Remember<S>2142</S> not the sins<S>2403</S> of my youth<S>5271</S>, nor my transgressions<S>6588</S>: according to thy mercy<S>2617</S> remember<S>2142</S> thou me for thy goodness'<S>2898</S> sake, O LORD<S>3068</S>.
KJV.8: Good<S>2896</S> and upright<S>3477</S> is the LORD<S>3068</S>: therefore will he teach<S>3384</S> sinners<S>2400</S> in the way<S>1870</S>.
KJV.9: The meek<S>6035</S> will he guide<S>1869</S> in judgment<S>4941</S>: and the meek<S>6035</S> will he teach<S>3925</S> his way<S>1870</S>.
KJV.10: All the paths<S>734</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> are mercy<S>2617</S> and truth<S>571</S> unto such as keep<S>5341</S> his covenant<S>1285</S> and his testimonies<S>5713</S>.
KJV.11: For thy name's<S>8034</S> sake, O LORD<S>3068</S>, pardon<S>5545</S> mine iniquity<S>5771</S>; for it is great<S>7227</S>.
KJV.12: What man<S>376</S> is he that feareth<S>3373</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>? him shall he teach<S>3384</S> in the way<S>1870</S> that he shall choose<S>977</S>.
KJV.13: His soul<S>5315</S> shall dwell<S>3885</S> at ease<S>2896</S>; and his seed<S>2233</S> shall inherit<S>3423</S> the earth<S>776</S>. <sup>dwell: Heb. lodge in goodness</sup>
KJV.14: The secret<S>5475</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> is with them that fear<S>3373</S> him; and he will shew<S>3045</S> them his covenant<S>1285</S>. <sup>and: or, and his covenant to make them know it</sup>
KJV.15: Mine eyes<S>5869</S> are ever<S>8548</S> toward the LORD<S>3068</S>; for he shall pluck<S>3318</S> my feet<S>7272</S> out of the net<S>7568</S>. <sup>pluck: Heb. bring forth</sup>
KJV.16: Turn<S>6437</S> thee unto me, and have mercy<S>2603</S> upon me; for I am desolate<S>3173</S> and afflicted<S>6041</S>.
KJV.17: The troubles<S>6869</S> of my heart<S>3824</S> are enlarged<S>7337</S>: O bring thou me out<S>3318</S> of my distresses<S>4691</S>.
KJV.18: Look<S>7200</S> upon mine affliction<S>6040</S> and my pain<S>5999</S>; and forgive<S>5375</S> all my sins<S>2403</S>.
KJV.19: Consider<S>7200</S> mine enemies<S>341</S>; for they are many<S>7231</S>; and they hate<S>8130</S> me with cruel<S>2555</S> hatred<S>8135</S>. <sup>cruel: Heb. hatred of violence</sup>
KJV.20: O keep<S>8104</S> my soul<S>5315</S>, and deliver<S>5337</S> me: let me not be ashamed<S>954</S>; for I put my trust<S>2620</S> in thee.
KJV.21: Let integrity<S>8537</S> and uprightness<S>3476</S> preserve<S>5341</S> me; for I wait<S>6960</S> on thee.
KJV.22: Redeem<S>6299</S> Israel<S>3478</S>, O God<S>430</S>, out of all his troubles<S>6869</S>.

ESV.1: To you, O LORD, I lift up my soul.
ESV.2: O my God, in you I trust; let me not be put to shame; let not my enemies exult over me.
ESV.3: Indeed, none who wait for you shall be put to shame; they shall be ashamed who are wantonly treacherous.
ESV.4: Make me to know your ways, O LORD; teach me your paths.
ESV.5: Lead me in your truth and teach me, for you are the God of my salvation; for you I wait all the day long.
ESV.6: Remember your mercy, O LORD, and your steadfast love, for they have been from of old.
ESV.7: Remember not the sins of my youth or my transgressions; according to your steadfast love remember me, for the sake of your goodness, O LORD!
ESV.8: Good and upright is the LORD; therefore he instructs sinners in the way.
ESV.9: He leads the humble in what is right, and teaches the humble his way.
ESV.10: All the paths of the LORD are steadfast love and faithfulness, for those who keep his covenant and his testimonies.
ESV.11: For your name's sake, O LORD, pardon my guilt, for it is great.
ESV.12: Who is the man who fears the LORD? Him will he instruct in the way that he should choose.
ESV.13: His soul shall abide in well-being, and his offspring shall inherit the land.
ESV.14: The friendship  of the LORD is for those who fear him, and he makes known to them his covenant.
ESV.15: My eyes are ever toward the LORD, for he will pluck my feet out of the net.
ESV.16: Turn to me and be gracious to me, for I am lonely and afflicted.
ESV.17: The troubles of my heart are enlarged; bring me out of my distresses.
ESV.18: Consider my affliction and my trouble, and forgive all my sins.
ESV.19: Consider how many are my foes, and with what violent hatred they hate me.
ESV.20: Oh, guard my soul, and deliver me! Let me not be put to shame, for I take refuge in you.
ESV.21: May integrity and uprightness preserve me, for I wait for you.
ESV.22: Redeem Israel, O God, out of all his troubles.

NASB.1: [A Psalm] of David. To You, O LORD, I lift up my soul.
NASB.2: O my God, in You I trust, Do not let me be ashamed; Do not let my enemies exult over me.
NASB.3: Indeed, none of those who wait for You will be ashamed; Those who deal treacherously without cause will be ashamed.
NASB.4: Make me know Your ways, O LORD; Teach me Your paths.
NASB.5: Lead me in Your truth and teach me, For You are the God of my salvation; For You I wait all the day.
NASB.6: Remember, O LORD, Your compassion and Your lovingkindnesses, For they have been from of old.
NASB.7: Do not remember the sins of my youth or my transgressions; According to Your lovingkindness remember me, For Your goodness' sake, O LORD.
NASB.8: Good and upright is the LORD; Therefore He instructs sinners in the way.
NASB.9: He leads the humble in justice, And He teaches the humble His way.
NASB.10: All the paths of the LORD are lovingkindness and truth To those who keep His covenant and His testimonies.
NASB.11: For Your name's sake, O LORD, Pardon my iniquity, for it is great.
NASB.12: Who is the man who fears the LORD? He will instruct him in the way he should choose.
NASB.13: His soul will abide in prosperity, And his descendants will inherit the land.
NASB.14: The secret of the LORD is for those who fear Him, And He will make them know His covenant.
NASB.15: My eyes are continually toward the LORD, For He will pluck my feet out of the net.
NASB.16: Turn to me and be gracious to me, For I am lonely and afflicted.
NASB.17: The troubles of my heart are enlarged; Bring me out of my distresses.
NASB.18: Look upon my affliction and my trouble, And forgive all my sins.
NASB.19: Look upon my enemies, for they are many, And they hate me with violent hatred.
NASB.20: Guard my soul and deliver me; Do not let me be ashamed, for I take refuge in You.
NASB.21: Let integrity and uprightness preserve me, For I wait for You.
NASB.22: Redeem Israel, O God, Out of all his troubles.

NLT.1: O LORD, I give my life to you.
NLT.2:
I trust in you, my God!
Do not let me be disgraced,
or let my enemies rejoice in my defeat.
NLT.3:
No one who trusts in you will ever be disgraced,
but disgrace comes to those who try to deceive others.
NLT.4:
Show me the right path, O LORD;
point out the road for me to follow.
NLT.5:
Lead me by your truth and teach me,
for you are the God who saves me.
All day long I put my hope in you.
NLT.6:
Remember, O LORD, your compassion and unfailing love,
which you have shown from long ages past.
NLT.7:
Do not remember the rebellious sins of my youth.
Remember me in the light of your unfailing love,
for you are merciful, O LORD.
NLT.8:
The LORD is good and does what is right;
he shows the proper path to those who go astray.
NLT.9:
He leads the humble in doing right,
teaching them his way.
NLT.10:
The LORD leads with unfailing love and faithfulness
all who keep his covenant and obey his demands.
NLT.11:
For the honor of your name, O LORD,
forgive my many, many sins.
NLT.12:
Who are those who fear the LORD?
He will show them the path they should choose.
NLT.13:
They will live in prosperity,
and their children will inherit the land.
NLT.14:
The LORD is a friend to those who fear him.
He teaches them his covenant.
NLT.15:
My eyes are always on the LORD,
for he rescues me from the traps of my enemies.
NLT.16:
Turn to me and have mercy,
for I am alone and in deep distress.
NLT.17:
My problems go from bad to worse.
Oh, save me from them all!
NLT.18:
Feel my pain and see my trouble.
Forgive all my sins.
NLT.19:
See how many enemies I have
and how viciously they hate me!
NLT.20:
Protect me! Rescue my life from them!
Do not let me be disgraced, for in you I take refuge.
NLT.21:
May integrity and honesty protect me,
for I put my hope in you.
NLT.22:
O God, ransom Israel
from all its troubles.

WEB.1: By David. To you, Yahweh, do I lift up my soul.
WEB.2: My God, I have trusted in you. Don’t let me be shamed. Don’t let my enemies triumph over me.
WEB.3: Yes, no one who waits for you shall be shamed. They shall be shamed who deal treacherously without cause.
WEB.4: Show me your ways, Yahweh. Teach me your paths.
WEB.5: Guide me in your truth, and teach me, For you are the God of my salvation, I wait for you all day long.
WEB.6: Yahweh, remember your tender mercies and your loving kindness, for they are from old times.
WEB.7: Don’t remember the sins of my youth, nor my transgressions. Remember me according to your loving kindness, for your goodness’ sake, Yahweh.
WEB.8: Good and upright is Yahweh, therefore he will instruct sinners in the way.
WEB.9: He will guide the humble in justice. He will teach the humble his way.
WEB.10: All the paths of Yahweh are loving kindness and truth to such as keep his covenant and his testimonies.
WEB.11: For your name’s sake, Yahweh, pardon my iniquity, for it is great.
WEB.12: What man is he who fears Yahweh? He shall instruct him in the way that he shall choose.
WEB.13: His soul shall dwell at ease. His offspring  shall inherit the land.
WEB.14: The friendship of Yahweh is with those who fear him. He will show them his covenant.
WEB.15: My eyes are ever on Yahweh, for he will pluck my feet out of the net.
WEB.16: Turn to me, and have mercy on me, for I am desolate and afflicted.
WEB.17: The troubles of my heart are enlarged. Oh bring me out of my distresses.
WEB.18: Consider my affliction and my travail. Forgive all my sins.
WEB.19: Consider my enemies, for they are many. They hate me with cruel hatred.
WEB.20: Oh keep my soul, and deliver me. Let me not be disappointed, for I take refuge in you.
WEB.21: Let integrity and uprightness preserve me, for I wait for you.
WEB.22: Redeem Israel, God, out all of his troubles.

CSB17.9:

CSB17.13:

CSB17.14:

AMP.1: A Psalm of David. To You, O LORD, I lift up my soul.
AMP.2:
O my God, in You I [have unwavering] trust [and I rely on You with steadfast confidence],
Do not let me be ashamed or my hope in You be disappointed;
Do not let my enemies triumph over me.
AMP.3:
Indeed, none of those who [expectantly] wait for You will be ashamed;
Those who turn away from what is right and deal treacherously without cause will be ashamed (humiliated, embarrassed).
AMP.4:
Let me know Your ways, O LORD;
Teach me Your paths.
AMP.5:
Guide me in Your truth and teach me,
For You are the God of my salvation;
For You [and only You] I wait [expectantly] all the day long.
AMP.6:
Remember, O LORD, Your [tender] compassion and Your lovingkindnesses,
For they have been from of old.
AMP.7:
Do not remember the sins of my youth or my transgressions;
According to Your lovingkindness remember me,
For Your goodness' sake, O LORD.
AMP.8:
Good and upright is the LORD;
Therefore He instructs sinners in the way.
AMP.9:
He leads the humble in justice,
And He teaches the humble His way.
AMP.10:
All the paths of the LORD are lovingkindness and goodness and truth and faithfulness
To those who keep His covenant and His testimonies.
AMP.11:
For Your name's sake, O LORD,
Pardon my wickedness and my guilt, for they are great.
AMP.12:
Who is the man who fears the LORD [with awe-inspired reverence and worships Him with submissive wonder]?
He will teach him [through His word] in the way he should choose.
AMP.13:
His soul will dwell in prosperity and goodness,
And his descendants will inherit the land.
AMP.14:
The secret [of the wise counsel] of the LORD is for those who fear Him,
And He will let them know His covenant and reveal to them [through His word] its [deep, inner] meaning.
AMP.15:
My eyes are continually toward the LORD,
For He will bring my feet out of the net.
AMP.16:
Turn to me [LORD] and be gracious to me,
For I am alone and afflicted.
AMP.17:
The troubles of my heart are multiplied;
Bring me out of my distresses.
AMP.18:
Look upon my affliction and my trouble,
And forgive all my sins.
AMP.19:
Look upon my enemies, for they are many;
They hate me with cruel and violent hatred.
AMP.20:
Guard my soul and rescue me;
Do not let me be ashamed or disappointed,
For I have taken refuge in You.
AMP.21:
Let integrity and uprightness protect me,
For I wait [expectantly] for You.
AMP.22:
O God, redeem Israel,
Out of all his troubles.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
My head is high, God, held high;
MSG.2:
I'm looking to you, God;
No hangdog skulking for me.
MSG.3:
I've thrown in my lot with you;
You won't embarrass me, will you?
Or let my enemies get the best of me?
Don't embarrass any of us
Who went out on a limb for you.
It's the traitors who should be humiliated.
MSG.4:
Show me how you work, God;
School me in your ways.
MSG.5:
Take me by the hand;
Lead me down the path of truth.
You are my Savior, aren't you?
MSG.6:
Mark the milestones of your mercy and love, God;
Rebuild the ancient landmarks!
MSG.7:
Forget that I sowed wild oats;
Mark me with your sign of love.
Plan only the best for me, God!
MSG.8:
God is fair and just;
He corrects the misdirected,
Sends them in the right direction.
MSG.9:
He gives the rejects his hand,
And leads them step-by-step.
MSG.10:
From now on every road you travel
Will take you to God.
Follow the Covenant signs;
Read the charted directions.
MSG.11:
Keep up your reputation, God;
Forgive my bad life;
It's been a very bad life.
MSG.12:
My question: What are God-worshipers like?
Your answer: Arrows aimed at God's bull's-eye.
MSG.13:
They settle down in a promising place;
Their kids inherit a prosperous farm.
MSG.14:
God-friendship is for God-worshipers;
They are the ones he confides in.
MSG.15:
If I keep my eyes on God,
I won't trip over my own feet.
MSG.16:
Look at me and help me!
I'm all alone and in big trouble.
MSG.17:
My heart and kidneys are fighting each other;
Call a truce to this civil war.
MSG.18:
Take a hard look at my life of hard labor,
Then lift this ton of sin.
MSG.19:
Do you see how many people
Have it in for me?
How viciously they hate me?
MSG.20:
Keep watch over me and keep me out of trouble;
Don't let me down when I run to you.
MSG.21:
Use all your skill to put me together;
I wait to see your finished product.
MSG.22:
God, give your people a break
From this run of bad luck.

NIV.1:
Psalm 25
Of David.
To you, O Lord, I lift up my soul;
NIV.2:
in you I trust, O my God.
Do not let me be put to shame,
nor let my enemies triumph over me.
NIV.3:
No one whose hope is in you
will ever be put to shame,
but they will be put to shame
who are treacherous without excuse.
NIV.4:
Show me your ways, O Lord,
teach me your paths;
NIV.5:
guide me in your truth and teach me,
for you are God my Savior,
and my hope is in you all day long.
NIV.6:
Remember, O Lord, your great mercy and love,
for they are from of old.
NIV.7:
Remember not the sins of my youth
and my rebellious ways;
according to your love remember me,
for you are good, O Lord.
NIV.8:
Good and upright is the Lord;
therefore he instructs sinners in his ways.
NIV.9:
He guides the humble in what is right
and teaches them his way.
NIV.10:
All the ways of the Lord are loving and faithful
for those who keep the demands of his covenant.
NIV.11:
For the sake of your name, O Lord,
forgive my iniquity, though it is great.
NIV.12:
Who, then, is the man that fears the Lord?
He will instruct him in the way chosen for him.
NIV.13:
He will spend his days in prosperity,
and his descendants will inherit the land.
NIV.14:
The Lord confides in those who fear him;
he makes his covenant known to them.
NIV.15:
My eyes are ever on the Lord,
for only he will release my feet from the snare.
NIV.16:
Turn to me and be gracious to me,
for I am lonely and afflicted.
NIV.17:
The troubles of my heart have multiplied;
free me from my anguish.
NIV.18:
Look upon my affliction and my distress
and take away all my sins.
NIV.19:
See how my enemies have increased
and how fiercely they hate me!
NIV.20:
Guard my life and rescue me;
let me not be put to shame,
for I take refuge in you.
NIV.21:
May integrity and uprightness protect me,
because my hope is in you.
NIV.22:
Redeem Israel, O God,
from all their troubles!

NRSVCE.19:

and with what violent hatred they hate me.
NRSVCE.20:
O guard my life, and deliver me;
do not let me be put to shame, for I take refuge in you.
NRSVCE.21:
May integrity and uprightness preserve me,
for I wait for you.
NRSVCE.22:
Redeem Israel, O God,
out of all its troubles.

NET.1: O LORD, I come before you in prayer.
NET.2: My God, I trust in you. Please do not let me be humiliated; do not let my enemies triumphantly rejoice over me!
NET.3: Certainly none who rely on you will be humiliated. Those who deal in treachery will be thwarted and humiliated.
NET.4: Make me understand your ways, O LORD! Teach me your paths!
NET.5: Guide me into your truth and teach me. For you are the God who delivers me; on you I rely all day long.
NET.6: Remember your compassionate and faithful deeds, O LORD, for you have always acted in this manner.
NET.7: Do not hold against me the sins of my youth or my rebellious acts! Because you are faithful to me, extend to me your favor, O LORD!
NET.8: The LORD is both kind and fair; that is why he teaches sinners the right way to live.
NET.9: May he show the humble what is right! May he teach the humble his way!
NET.10: The LORD always proves faithful and reliable to those who follow the demands of his covenant.
NET.11: For the sake of your reputation, O LORD, forgive my sin, because it is great.
NET.12: The LORD shows his faithful followers the way they should live.
NET.13: They experience his favor; their descendants inherit the land.
NET.14: The LORD’s loyal followers receive his guidance, and he reveals his covenantal demands to them.
NET.15: I continually look to the LORD for help, for he will free my feet from the enemy’s net.
NET.16: Turn toward me and have mercy on me, for I am alone and oppressed!
NET.17: Deliver me from my distress; rescue me from my suffering!
NET.18: See my pain and suffering! Forgive all my sins!
NET.19: Watch my enemies, for they outnumber me; they hate me and want to harm me.
NET.20: Protect me and deliver me! Please do not let me be humiliated, for I have taken shelter in you!
NET.21: May integrity and godliness protect me, for I rely on you!
NET.22: O God, rescue Israel from all their distress!
